task_completion.description: Enable workspace-specific WFS settings for the 'ne' workspace
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the GeoServer web interface, on the WFS settings page for the 'ne' workspace, the 'Enable' checkbox is checked.

task_completion.description: Configure the WFS Title and Abstract
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Title' field contains exactly 'Natural Earth Feature Service' and the 'Abstract' field contains exactly 'Provides controlled access to Natural Earth vector datasets'.

task_completion.description: Configure the Service Level and Max Features
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Service Level' dropdown is set to 'COMPLETE' and the 'Maximum number of features' field is set to '50'.
A success message indicating the workspace/WFS settings were saved, or the agent clicking the 'Save' button at the bottom of the configuration page.

task_completion.description: Save the WFS GetCapabilities response to the home directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing a successful curl/wget command saving to 'ne_wfs_capabilities.xml', or a file manager/terminal 'ls' showing the file exists in '/home/ga/'.

task_completion.description: Save the WFS GetFeature response for 'ne_countries' to the home directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing a successful curl/wget command saving to 'ne_countries_features.json', or a file manager/terminal 'ls' showing the file exists in '/home/ga/'.

integrity.description: Agent used the GeoServer web interface to configure settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the actual GeoServer web administration GUI (http://localhost:8080/geoserver), not just editing raw XML configuration files in a text editor.

integrity.description: Agent made genuine HTTP requests to get the WFS responses
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should use tools like curl, wget, or a web browser to request the GetCapabilities and GetFeature endpoints from GeoServer, rather than manually creating files and typing fake content.

integrity.description: The response files were actually saved to disk
integrity.visual_evidence: The XML and JSON files must be saved to the filesystem (e.g., using output redirection '>' or '-o' in curl/wget, or 'Save As' in a browser), not just printed to the terminal stdout or viewed in a browser tab.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected WFS configuration for the 'ne' workspace must have Title: 'Natural Earth Feature Service', Abstract: 'Provides controlled access to Natural Earth vector datasets', and Max Features: 50. The GetCapabilities response must be saved to '/home/ga/ne_wfs_capabilities.xml' and the GetFeature response to '/home/ga/ne_countries_features.json'.
